title: Tieghemia rogersii
taxonomy: Tieghemia rogersii is a plant species classified within the family Podocarpaceae. It belongs to the genus Tieghemia, which is a monotypic genus. Further classification places it in the order Coniferales, class Pinopsida, and division Pinophyta. As a conifer, it is closely related to other members of the Podocarpaceae family. This classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.
parts: Tieghemia rogersii is a plant species that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. intro: Tieghemia rogersii, also known as Mexican evening primrose, is a species of flowering plant in the primrose family (Onagraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving skin health due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Tieghemia rogersii include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
